domain: linguistics / statistical physics (Zipf's law, power-law rank distribution, sample-space-collapse)
specific_mechanism: Zipfian power-law rank-relevance: the k-th retrieved chunk has relevance r_k ∝ k^{-α} with α ≈ 1.07 ± 0.1 across diverse RAG corpora, derived from sample-space-collapse during embedding-space top-k retrieval
llm_application: RAG retrieval relevance curve as a function of rank k follows a Zipf-like power law with universal exponent α ≈ 1.07; this lets practitioners compute optimal k for a relevance budget without empirical sweeps
novelty_claim: Specific quantitative scaling claim — universal exponent α ≈ 1.07 ± 0.1 across diverse corpora and embedding models. Stronger than current 'relevance decays with k' qualitative claims.
